'''[[Lady/Legends|Lady]] Theala Vandron''' was the name of both the wife and daughter of [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Crueya Vandron/Legends|Crueya Vandron]], the founder of [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] and head of [[House Vandron]], considered the eldest of the Ancient Houses of [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], and thus the Galaxy's senior aristocratic lineage by precedent.

The elder Lady Theala Vandron had married her husband by [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], in which year she served as the Senex sector ambassador to the [[Eriadu Trade Summit]]; her [[Theala Vandron Jr.|daughter]] was born within a few years, and by [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], she had succeeded her father as head of House Vandron. In that year, the younger Theala Vandron became embroiled in [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren's]] plot to establish her son [[Irek Ismaren|Irek]] as Emperor with the firepower of the ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'' behind him.

{{Char-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The distinction between the Lady Theala Vandron of ''Cloak of Deception'' and the Lady [[Theala Vandron Jr.|Theala Vandron]] of ''Children of the Jedi'' is necessitated by the fact that the former is a grown woman in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]] while the latter is still in her forties forty-six years later. Additionally, it should be noted that while the Lady Theala Vandron of ''Cloak of Deception'' seems merely to be married to the Lord Vandron of the time, the Lady Theala Vandron of ''Children of the Jedi'' is herself Lady Vandron, Head of the House in her own right.
